name: Braciole Il
recipeIngredient: 2 cups chopped canned pears with their juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the butterflied steak evenly with waxed paper covering it until approximately 1/4 inch thick.
2. Rub lemon rind, salt, pepper, and 1-1/2 tsp oregano into the meat.
3. Lay prosciutto slices evenly on the steak.
4. Sprinkle bread crumbs, grated Parmesan, and parsley over the prosciutto slices.
5. Roll the braciole tightly, tucking in both ends to hold the filling while cooking.
6. Tie the roll with strings at 1-1/2 to 2 inches intervals.
7. Rub rosemary between palms to break it up. Season flour mixture with remaining oregano, salt, and pepper.
8. Rub this mixture onto the surface of the beef roll.
9. Heat olive oil in a large pan over moderately high heat.
10. Add the beef roll, turning until browned on all sides.
11. Add garlic and onion, cooking until garlic turns golden brown.
12. Add wine and cook for one minute.
13. Add tomatoes with their juice and season with salt and pepper to taste.
14. Cover the pan and simmer over low heat for 1 to 1-1/2 hours or until tender when pierced with a fork.
15. Remove from pan, place on heated platter, remove strings, cut into 1/2-inch thick slices, and pour tomato mixture over the slices.
16. Serve immediately. If serving cold, cover tightly with plastic wrap or foil and refrigerate. Before serving, bring to room temperature, remove strings, and cut into slices as mentioned previously. Slices can be served on a bed of lettuce accompanied by freshly baked bread.
